title: From a church basement to M Street
dek: How the Preparatory High School for Colored Youth began under Fifteenth Street Presbyterian and became the school Washington would later call Dunbar.

paragraphs: In 1870, William Syphax and the Board of Trustees for Colored Schools opened a public high school for Black students in the basement of Fifteenth Street Presbyterian Church.

body.heading: A name that moved with the school
paragraphs: By 1891 the school had outgrown the basement identity. Renamed M Street High School, it carried the same institutional thread.
